broadcast.text: Aubrey kicks sixty-yarders like extra points. Special teams solved, one lottery ticket left to buy. Beautiful draft, no notes.
broadcast.speaker: homer

broadcast.text: Seven defenses came off the board in nine picks, which tells you exactly how much anybody trusts their week-one waiver plans.
broadcast.speaker: anchor

broadcast.text: Six running backs, five receivers, no holes. All that's left is picking a leg and a defense. Championship blueprint, folks.
broadcast.speaker: homer

broadcast.text: Tyreek Hill in the twelfth round is the strangest sentence anyone's said all night, and the room just kept moving.
broadcast.speaker: anchor

broadcast.text: White catches sixty balls a year in his sleep. That's a flex play in December when everybody's hurt. Value shopping, baby.
broadcast.speaker: homer

broadcast.text: We've hit the part of the draft where half the room is picking backup quarterbacks they'll cut by week three.
broadcast.speaker: anchor

broadcast.text: Downs catches everything thrown within a zip code of him. Four-deep at receiver with two flex spots? That's chess, folks.
broadcast.speaker: homer

broadcast.text: Quarterback benches are filling up with guys who were starters in September headlines — meanwhile actual lineup depth sits there unclaimed.
broadcast.speaker: anchor

broadcast.text: Aaron Jones has forgotten more football than half this draft board ever knew. Depth chart's starting to look like a fortress.
broadcast.speaker: homer

broadcast.text: The middle rounds are a used-car lot now — the smart shoppers are grabbing certified pre-owned running backs with service records.
broadcast.speaker: anchor

broadcast.text: Price at 101 is a steal by two full rounds. Bench guys with upside win Novembers, write it down.
broadcast.speaker: homer

broadcast.text: Round nine and the board's gone flat — this is where drafts are won by whoever still has a value list instead of a vibe.
broadcast.speaker: anchor

broadcast.text: The name alone starts in this family's living room, and now he's flex depth. Round eight! Somebody pinch the draft room.
broadcast.speaker: homer

broadcast.text: Marvin Harrison Junior in round eight. Last year's ADP would like a word.
broadcast.speaker: anchor

broadcast.text: Dak with a bye week that doesn't touch a single one of our guys? That's not a pick, that's a jigsaw piece.
broadcast.speaker: homer

broadcast.text: The quarterback shelf is one run from bare — Herbert's exit puts every QB-less roster on the clock, whether they know it or not.
broadcast.speaker: anchor

broadcast.text: Five picks, five starters. Now we're shopping for the bench and everybody else is panic-buying quarterbacks. Love our seat.
broadcast.speaker: homer

broadcast.text: Three quarterbacks went in six picks and the room still left Herbert, Prescott, and Lawrence on the table. Patience pays at the position everyone panics about.
broadcast.speaker: anchor

broadcast.text: Loveland was the grown-up pick and now we plug the backfield. Swift's boring. Boring wins Tuesdays.
broadcast.speaker: homer

broadcast.text: Middle rounds now and the quarterbacks are dangling — Burrow at fifty-three is the kind of thing that decides leagues in December.
broadcast.speaker: anchor

broadcast.text: Three wideouts in four rounds and every one of them was the best player sitting there. You take the gifts, you say thank you.
broadcast.speaker: homer

broadcast.text: Quarterbacks finally moved — Maye and Jackson back to back — and the room let DeVonta Smith fall out of the thirties.
broadcast.speaker: anchor

broadcast.text: You don't pass a top-17 talent at pick 29 over a bye week, people. That's coupon-clipping at the caviar counter.
broadcast.speaker: homer

broadcast.text: A.J. Brown sliding to the back of round three is the first real board break of the night. Somebody's getting a discount.
broadcast.speaker: anchor

broadcast.text: Puka plus Chase Brown through two rounds — that's a foundation, folks. Byes 11 and 6, no stacking problem, no panic.
broadcast.speaker: homer

broadcast.text: Nine running backs went in the first nineteen picks. If you wanted one, the shelf is down to Chase Brown and hope.
broadcast.speaker: anchor

broadcast.text: Puka to the good guys, book it. Best player available AND he doesn't share a bye with anybody we care about. Draft room's cooking.
broadcast.speaker: homer

broadcast.text: Four picks in and the running back run is already on — Robinson, McCaffrey, Gibbs gone. The board says the value pick here is a wideout.
broadcast.speaker: anchor
